title: American Oversight Records Confirm ICE Trained Agents on 4th Amendment, Then Told to Ignore It
excerpt: Documents obtained through a FOIA lawsuit corroborated whistleblower Ryan Schwank's congressional testimony, showing ICE's own July 2025 training materials instructed agents they could not enter homes without a judicial warrant—while instructors were simultaneously directed to tell cadets the policy was 'under review' and to follow a contradictory memo from Acting ICE Director Todd Lyons.
content:
On February 26, 2026, American Oversight published ICE training materials obtained through a FOIA lawsuit that directly corroborated the earlier congressional testimony of former ICE attorney and whistleblower Ryan Schwank.

  • ICE's 'Fourth Amendment Refresher Training (Revised July 2025)' and accompanying instructor notes explicitly stated agents generally cannot enter a residence without a judicial warrant or consent, and that administrative warrants do not authorize home entry.
  • Despite this formal training, instructors were directed to tell cadets that the use of administrative warrants to enter homes was 'under review'—a directive instructors were told to deliver verbally and not document in writing.
  • The undocumented directive aligned with a memo from Acting ICE Director Todd Lyons that Schwank testified claimed ICE officers could enter homes without a judicial warrant.
  • Schwank testified that Lyons' memo 'authorized the very conduct that DHS in its own 2025 legal training materials had called the chief evil against which the wording of the Fourth Amendment is directed—that is, physical entry of the home.'
  • American Oversight Executive Director Chioma Chukwu stated: 'These records bolster the whistleblower's claims and suggest a troubling disconnect between ICE's formal legal training and the way ICE officers were instructed to enter homes without warrants.'
mediaPlaceholder: ICE Fourth Amendment Refresher Training materials (July 2025) showing gap between written guidance and verbal instructor directives
